trigger: Aug 2024: NIST finalizes FIPS 203/204/205 — world's first PQC standards. Mar 2026: Trump Cyber Strategy mandates PQC alongside zero-trust. "Harvest now, decrypt later" attacks confirmed active by US intelligence — any encrypted data stored today is a future quantum target.

timeline.event: NIST launches global competition for quantum-resistant algorithms, receiving 82 initial proposals from 25 countries — the largest open cryptography standardization effort in history.
NIST announces 4 finalists: CRYSTALS-Kyber (key encapsulation), CRYSTALS-Dilithium, FALCON, SPHINCS+ (signatures). Industry migration planning begins in earnest.

NIST finalizes FIPS 203 (ML-KEM), FIPS 204 (ML-DSA), FIPS 205 (SLH-DSA) — 8-year competition concludes. Google's Willow quantum chip accelerates Q-Day timeline estimates. Enterprise CBOM audits launch across Fortune 500.

US OMB issues binding migration timelines for all federal agencies under M-23-02. SandboxAQ closes $300M+ raise at $5.75B valuation. Quantinuum raises $600M from investors including Nvidia at $10B valuation — PQC becomes a venture darling.

EU ENISA publishes Coordinated PQC Implementation Roadmap with phased milestones: 2026 inventory, 2030 migration, 2035 full harmonisation. UAE issues binding National Encryption Policy requiring 2026 migration plan submissions — first government with a hard deadline.

Trump Cyber Strategy explicitly mandates PQC adoption for all federal systems. PQC market hits $2.18B — up 38% YoY. Cloudflare and Chrome ship ML-KEM hybrid TLS as default for 5%+ of global HTTPS traffic. BFSI sector CNSA 2.0 deadline pressure triggers wave of enterprise migrations.

pestal.impact: Three enacted US federal statutes underpin PQC mandates — immune to executive-order reversal. Trump's March 2026 Cyber Strategy explicitly names PQC as a national security priority alongside zero-trust. CNSA 2.0 requires all National Security Systems to migrate to quantum-resistant algorithms by 2030. UAE is the first nation to issue binding migration submission requirements in 2026.

Market grows from $1.58B (2025) to $20.5B by 2033 at 37.8% CAGR. NXP, Thales, AWS, Palo Alto, and IDEMIA hold ~60% combined share. SandboxAQ ($5.75B valuation) and Quantinuum ($10B, Nvidia-backed) signal institutional capital is now flowing at scale. PQC migration consulting will outpace the software market 3x through 2028.

"Harvest now, decrypt later" attacks confirmed active by US intelligence agencies — adversaries are storing today's encrypted traffic for future quantum decryption. Healthcare, financial, and government data with 10+ year sensitivity windows is the primary target. CISOs increasingly citing PQC as a board-level risk, not a future-state R&D project.

NIST FIPS 203/204/205 eliminate the 'waiting for standards' blocker as of Aug 2024. OpenSSL 3.2, Chrome 124+, Cloudflare, and AWS now support ML-KEM hybrid TLS. Google's Willow chip (Dec 2024) demonstrated error-corrected quantum computing at scale — shortening credible Q-Day estimates from 2030–2035 to a broader 2028–2032 window.

Lattice-based PQC algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA) require 2–4x more CPU cycles per operation than RSA-2048, increasing server energy consumption during the transition period. Larger key and signature sizes (ML-KEM-768 keys are ~1KB vs RSA-2048's 256 bytes) raise network bandwidth and certificate storage costs — material at hyperscale. [unverified quantification of total energy delta]

FIPS 203/204/205 are now mandatory for US federal procurement. CNSA 2.0 sets hard NSS migration deadlines through 2030. EU ENISA roadmap targets 2030 harmonization, with GDPR's long-lived data retention creating de facto liability for failure to migrate before Q-Day. Basel Committee quantum risk guidance adds BFSI regulatory pressure. Companies storing HIPAA-protected data for 10+ years face retroactive liability risk.

jobs.job: Protect web traffic and APIs from harvest-now-decrypt-later adversaries storing TLS sessions today for future quantum decryption
jobs.solution: Deploy ML-KEM hybrid key encapsulation in TLS 1.3 — already available in OpenSSL 3.2, Chrome, Cloudflare, and AWS ACM. Hybrid mode (classical + PQC) provides quantum resistance with zero backwards-compatibility breakage.

jobs.job: Audit and inventory all cryptographic dependencies before regulatory deadlines without requiring cryptography expertise in every team
jobs.solution: Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) scanners such as SandboxAQ AQtive Guard and IBM Guardium Quantum Safe that auto-detect RSA/ECC usage across codebases, infrastructure, and APIs — producing a prioritized migration roadmap in days, not months.

jobs.job: Migrate enterprise PKI — code-signing certificates, internal CAs, identity tokens — before the 2030 CNSA deadline
jobs.solution: Crypto-agile certificate lifecycle management platforms (Keyfactor, Venafi, AppViewX) that automate dual-issue (classical + PQC hybrid) certificates and track expiry across thousands of endpoints — replacing manual PKI management that breaks under PQC key-size expansion.

jobs.job: Secure IoT and embedded devices with constrained compute that cannot run standard PQC algorithms
jobs.solution: Lightweight PQC hardware IPs such as PQShield's embedded cores and NXP's quantum-resistant secure elements, delivering SLH-DSA signatures at <10mW — enabling PQC in payment cards, automotive ECUs, and industrial sensors without a hardware redesign.

jobs.job: Prove compliance to regulators (CNSA 2.0, ENISA, Basel) and auditors who need documented evidence of PQC migration progress
jobs.solution: Compliance-mapped PQC dashboards that link every cryptographic asset to its regulatory obligation and migration status — converting the CBOM inventory into a real-time audit trail acceptable to NIST NCCoE, EU NIS Cooperation Group, and financial regulators.

market.name: NXP Semiconductor
market.loved: Broadest hardware footprint from automotive ECUs to payment cards; embedding ML-KEM in secure elements shipped to 2B+ endpoints annually; semiconductor-level PQC unavoidable if chip supply is involved

market.name: Thales
market.loved: Luna HSM fleet (most-deployed enterprise HSM) getting PQC firmware updates; key management layer that enterprises already trust for long-lived key storage is the migration gateway

market.name: AWS (KMS / ACM)
market.loved: Hybrid PQC in AWS Certificate Manager and IoT Core since 2025; largest cloud provider making PQC the path-of-least-resistance default for AWS-native workloads

market.name: SandboxAQ
market.loved: $1B+ raised; Alphabet spinout; AQtive Guard is the market-leading enterprise CBOM + migration platform; preferred vendor for US DoD, financial institutions, and telecom operators

market.name: IBM Quantum Safe
market.loved: Designed CRYSTALS-Kyber (now ML-KEM) — the core NIST standard; Guardium Quantum Safe + open-source CBOM tooling; IBM Consulting migration services targeting Fortune 1000

competitors.name: SandboxAQ
competitors.detail: Spun from Alphabet; $1B+ raised, $5.75B valuation. AQtive Guard platform scans enterprise infrastructure for cryptographic risk and generates a prioritized migration roadmap. Works with US DoD, major banks, and global telecom operators. Largest pure-play PQC migration platform by revenue.

competitors.name: PQShield (UK)
competitors.detail: $63M+ raised; UK Tech Nation Future Fifty 2026 member. Sells hardware and software PQC IP cores for embedding in chips, firmware, and applications. Supplies semiconductor vendors and government agencies. Specialises in NIST-compliant implementations optimised for constrained IoT hardware.

competitors.name: Quantinuum
competitors.detail: Formed from Honeywell Quantum Solutions + Cambridge Quantum merger (2021). Raised $600M at $10B valuation in 2024 — Nvidia's VC arm invested. InQuanto platform bridges PQC research and enterprise deployment. Filed confidential S-1 for what could be the sector's largest IPO.

competitors.name: IBM Quantum Safe
competitors.detail: Designed CRYSTALS-Kyber (ML-KEM) — the primary NIST PQC key-encapsulation standard. Guardium Quantum Safe provides CBOM scanning and migration orchestration for enterprise clients. Open-sourced CBOM tooling to establish industry standard. IBM Consulting offers end-to-end migration engagements for Fortune 1000.

competitors.name: Cloudflare / Google
competitors.detail: Not vendors but de-facto standard setters. Cloudflare deployed ML-KEM hybrid TLS for all traffic by Q1 2026. Google's Chrome 124+ enabled post-quantum key agreement by default. Together they are migrating 5%+ of global HTTPS traffic and forcing browser compatibility — making hybrid PQC the path-of-least-resistance for every web service.

canvas.who: CISOs, security architects, and IT compliance officers at enterprises holding long-lived sensitive data; US federal agencies and defense contractors under CNSA 2.0 mandates; BFSI firms under Basel Committee quantum risk guidance and DORA; healthcare organizations with HIPAA 10-year retention requirements; telecom operators and critical infrastructure operators under NIS2/ENISA roadmap obligations
canvas.where: United States (CNSA 2.0 hardest deadline — NSS migration by 2030), UAE (first binding 2026 migration plan submission), EU (ENISA roadmap + GDPR liability), Asia-Pacific (fastest-growing adoption driven by national security programs); predominantly enterprise software, cloud services, and hardware security module upgrades
canvas.why_now: NIST FIPS 203/204/205 finalized August 2024 eliminated the 'waiting for standards' excuse. Google's Willow chip compressed credible Q-Day estimates. 'Harvest now, decrypt later' attacks are confirmed active — intelligence agencies in the US and UK have publicly warned encrypted data is being collected today for future decryption. Enterprise migrations take 5–15 years depending on complexity, meaning a 2026 start is already the minimum viable timeline to meet 2030 federal mandates.
canvas.behavior: Enterprises launching Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) audits to inventory every RSA/ECC dependency across codebases and infrastructure. Cloud providers shipping hybrid PQC as the default TLS configuration, making opt-in obsolete. Government procurement RFPs in 2026 requiring PQC compliance documentation as a vendor qualification threshold. Certificate authorities dual-issuing classical + PQC hybrid certificates during transition. Security budgets shifting from network perimeter tools to cryptographic infrastructure modernization.
canvas.unmet: Migration tooling that works at enterprise scale without requiring cryptography expertise in every development team — current CBOM scanners still require specialist interpretation. Backwards-compatible hybrid PQC that doesn't break legacy endpoints or embedded devices deployed for 10-20 year lifespans. Regulatory clarity on liability: who bears legal responsibility if pre-migration encrypted data is decrypted post-Q-Day when the company met all current compliance deadlines. Performance benchmarks and cost modeling for PQC at hyperscale — enterprises cannot justify migration spend without quantified latency and energy overhead data.

takeaways: The migration clock started in August 2024 — every quarter without a Cryptographic Bill of Materials audit is compounding technical debt. Enterprises that launch CBOM projects in 2026 can realistically complete migrations before 2031 CNSA deadlines; those that wait until 2028 will miss federal mandates. The single most valuable thing any security team can do today is run a CBOM scan and produce a prioritized migration roadmap.

'Harvest now, decrypt later' is the most underpriced enterprise risk of 2026. Any data with a 7+ year sensitivity horizon — health records, financial transaction history, classified communications, IP — should be treated as already under collection by quantum-capable adversaries within the decade. Vendors who can translate this into CFO-legible financial exposure estimates will win security budget that no compliance mandate alone can unlock.

Build for crypto-agility, not a one-time CRYSTALS-Kyber swap. NIST may issue FIPS revisions as quantum hardware evolves; systems that hardcode a single algorithm will require full re-engineering within 5 years. Designing with algorithm-negotiation layers (like TLS cipher suites) from the start is the difference between a migration and a rebuild — price it accordingly in architecture decisions.

The highest-velocity near-term market is BFSI and federal verticals — combined CNSA 2.0, Basel Committee, and DORA pressure creates a mandatory-spend forcing function that no other sector faces yet. PQC compliance consulting, managed migration services, and crypto-agility audit tooling targeting these two segments will outgrow the underlying technology market by 3x through 2028. Position now before the wave of government contract awards in H2 2026.
